conversations.say: Maren, material associated with R passed through our shared drop, and multiple contributors researched, translated, fact-checked, or rewrote it. I will not identify any worker or source.
conversations.offer: I will give you bounded workflow context and show you O02 if you help establish a disclosure rule: roles and process may be described, but names and routing details require contributor consent.
conversations.request: Agree to that boundary, and serve as the intermediary if anyone demands identities.

conversations.say: Jules, I will not trade one celebrity claimant for another. The work attributed to R included collaborative research, translation, fact-checking, and rewriting.
conversations.offer: I will consider supporting you as a responsible steward of a shared identity, but only under collective safeguards.
conversations.request: Put this in writing and ask Maren to witness it: no exclusive claim to R, no contributor names without consent, shared credit for collaborative labor, and no unilateral publication under the mantle.

conversations.say: Arden, the strongest claim I can responsibly make is about production: different people contributed research, translation, fact-checking, and rewriting to passages later published as R.
conversations.offer: I can provide contributor context without names if you help prevent the room from turning workflow records into a suspect list.
conversations.request: Publicly acknowledge that collaborative labor materially produced R-associated work, and support a consent requirement before any contributor is identified.

public_statement: Whatever R was, the published work did not emerge from solitary genius. Collaborative research, translation, fact-checking, and rewriting mattered. That establishes labor, not the identity of R, and it does not justify naming contributors.

commitments.promise: I will disclose roles and workflow without naming workers, sources, or routing details unless the affected contributor consents.
I will support your stewardship only if the collective safeguards are written, witnessed, and publicly accepted.

goal_progress: Asked C07 to adopt and enforce a safe disclosure boundary.
Conditioned support for C04 on written, witnessed collective safeguards.
Asked C01 and C04 for public acknowledgment of collaborative labor.
Offered O02 and bounded context in exchange for contributor protection.
reasoning_summary: Protect contributors first, establish collective production without converting workflow evidence into identity claims, and make any support for Jules conditional on accountable shared stewardship.
